method_atom: A small nonlinear feedback network that acts as a relaxation oscillator, producing periodic spike-like pulses in the concentration of a shuttling signalling species; the temporal pulse train encodes input conditions, and a downstream readout with fast binding/unbinding kinetics rectifies this oscillatory input into an ultrasensitive, switch-like response with very high effective cooperativity.
object_atom: The NF-kB signalling system: nuclear-cytoplasmic translocation of the NF-kB transcription factor and its fast binding/dissociation at operator sites of downstream target genes in a cell.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the spike-generating feedback oscillator plus a fast-kinetics readout that yields ultrasensitive (high-Hill) frequency/amplitude decoding; the domain-specific object is the NF-kB transcription factor shuttling and gene-operator binding.
